mnemonics: Pipe Dreams Flow Downhill: Pipes (|) take the standard output (stdout) of one command and feed it as standard input (stdin) to the next, like a stream flowing downhill.
grep Gets Regular Expressions to Print: Use grep with regular expressions to search and print matching lines.
keyPoints: A pipe (|) connects the stdout of the left command to the stdin of the right command.
Basic Regular Expressions (BRE) use a simpler syntax (e.g., grep uses . * [ ] ^ $ by default).
Extended Regular Expressions (ERE) use a more powerful syntax (e.g., grep -E uses + ? | ( ) { } for more complex patterns).
commonMistakes: Forgetting that pipes only handle stdout; stderr is not passed through by default and must be redirected (2>&1).
Using ERE metacharacters (like +, |, ?) with basic grep without enabling extended regex with the -E flag, which causes them to be interpreted as literal characters.
